content:
MODULE: cosmos.integration.hackathon.quantum_proof
FILE: cosmos/integration/hackathon/quantum_proof.py (414 lines)
CATEGORY: integration

DESCRIPTION: QUANTUM PROOF - Real IBM Quantum Hardware Execution
====================================================

Submits actual quantum circuits to IBM Quantum hardware.
Jobs appear in IBM Quantum portal under "Past Workloads".

Uses minimal qubit/shot counts to conserve quota while
demonstrating real quan

CLASSES:
- QuantumJob: A quantum job submitted to IBM hardware.
- QuantumProof: Real IBM Quantum hardware integration.

Submits actual quantum circuits to demon
  Methods: async def initialize(self) -> bool
  Methods: def get_least_busy_backend(self) -> Optional[str]
  Methods: async def run_bell_state(self, shots: int=100, backend: Optional[str]=None) -> QuantumJob
  Methods: async def run_quantum_random(self, num_bits: int=8, shots: int=1, backend: Optional[str]=None) -> Qu
  Methods: async def run_ghz_state(self, num_qubits: int=3, shots: int=100, backend: Optional[str]=None) -> Qua
  Methods: async def get_job_status(self, job_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]
  Methods: async def wait_for_result(self, job_id: str, timeout: float=300.0, poll_interval: float=5.0) -> Dict
  Methods: def get_jobs(self) -> list

PUBLIC FUNCTIONS:
- def get_quantum_proof() -> QuantumProof
- async def run_quantum_bell(shots: int=100) -> Dict[str, Any]
- async def run_quantum_random_bits(num_bits: int=8) -> Dict[str, Any]
